note: Verbatim phrases of the locked narration, in spoken order, split at the script's break tags and sentence boundaries into caption-sized pieces. Caption text is never rewritten, summarized, or reordered. Block windows are the measured onsets of each rendered block inside the joined master (normalized cross-correlation of each WC-02-03-sNN.mp3 against WC-02-03-full-narration-v1.0.mp3 at 8 kHz; s06 verified with a mid-block template, ncc 1.0); the theoretical audio+0.4 tail model ends at 148.48s against the ffprobe master duration of 148.114s (0.366s over) because the joined master uses ~0.36s inter-block gaps, so measured onsets are used for accurate phrase placement.

phrases.text: Open any customer's profile and click Edit Customer.
The same record opens ready to change, every field now live.
Two buttons wait in the corner.
One saves your work, one backs out.
Today, we only back out.
Before anything else, read the band across the top.
The name, the plan, and a few status chips ride along from the profile.
It is your proof of whose record you are in, so you never edit the wrong person by accident.
Now the tabs.
Six here, not the nine on the profile.
The view is built for reading a customer;
this form is built for changing one, so the layout shifts.
Messaging turns into Communication, Notes into Comments,
and the read-only summaries fall away.
Start on Contact, the first group.
Name, company, gender, date of birth,
plus two switches for house account and signature on file.
This is the identity block, the details that decide how the customer shows up everywhere else.
Next, the address fields.
Street, city, state, zip, and country, with a second line for an apartment or suite.
Keep these clean and the customer's mail, receipts, and location reports all line up.
Below that, phone and email.
Phone, mobile, fax, and email address.
When a member says their texts or receipts are going nowhere,
this is the block you check first.
We changed nothing, so click Cancel.
The form closes and drops you right back on the profile, untouched.
That Save button would have written straight to a live account,
so while you are learning, Cancel is the only way out.
Head back to the customer list.
Same database you started in.
Up top sit the counts, and along the toolbar, the button that starts a brand new record.
Click Add Customer.
The form looks familiar because it is the same one, just empty.
Same tabs, same fields, every box blank and waiting.
This is where a walk-in becomes a member.
Scan down the empty form.
Personal details, address, phone and email, all in the same order as the edit screen.
Learn the layout once and you know both, whether you are fixing a record or building one.
We are only touring, so click Cancel again.
No record is created, and nothing is left behind.
Two forms, one template, and the same safe habit both times:
read everything, change nothing, leave by Cancel.
